labs.name: Introduction to AI and Machine Learning on Google Cloud
labs.exam_yield_note: Single best on-ramp to GCP's AI portfolio for true beginners — covers Vertex AI, BigQuery ML, Model Garden, AutoML, Gemini in one sitting.

labs.name: Prepare Data for ML APIs on Google Cloud
labs.exam_yield_note: Skill-badge challenge lab forces hands-on Dataprep/Dataflow/ML APIs — cheapest way to internalise §1.2 ML APIs sub-domain.

labs.name: Working with Notebooks in Vertex AI
labs.exam_yield_note: §2.2 'Model prototyping using Jupyter notebooks' is an exam-guide bullet — Workbench/Colab Enterprise mechanics matter.

labs.name: Create ML Models with BigQuery ML
labs.exam_yield_note: §1.1 is entirely BQML; the challenge lab is the fastest way to get the SQL pattern (CREATE MODEL / ML.PREDICT) into muscle memory.

labs.name: Engineer Data for Predictive Modeling with BigQuery ML
labs.exam_yield_note: Reinforces §1.1 + §2.1 (Dataflow/BQ ETL); skip if comfortable after #4.

labs.name: Feature Engineering
labs.exam_yield_note: Vertex AI Feature Store appears explicitly in §2.1 and §2.2 — only deep treatment in the path. UI mismatch likely; concepts current.

labs.name: Build, Train and Deploy ML Models with Keras on Google Cloud
labs.exam_yield_note: TF/Keras + Vertex AI deployment hits §3.1, §3.2, §4.1 simultaneously. Longest course in the path; carries the most weight per hour.

labs.name: Production Machine Learning Systems
labs.exam_yield_note: Static vs dynamic training/inference, batch vs online — these distinctions appear verbatim in exam questions.

labs.name: Machine Learning Operations (MLOps): Getting Started
labs.exam_yield_note: Sets vocabulary (CT/CI/CD, drift, serving signature) used across §5 and §6. Very short, very high ROI.

labs.name: MLOps with Vertex AI: Manage Features
labs.exam_yield_note: Streaming ingestion to Feature Store is niche but explicitly enumerated in the exam guide §2.1.

labs.name: Introduction to Generative AI
console_focus: Vertex AI Studio (now Agent Platform Studio)
labs.exam_yield_note: v3.1 update explicitly added 'tasks related to generative AI' — cheapest unit to satisfy that bullet.

labs.name: Introduction to Large Language Models
labs.exam_yield_note: 10-minute survey; useful only if zero LLM exposure.

labs.name: Machine Learning Operations (MLOps) for Generative AI
labs.exam_yield_note: Gen-AI MLOps (prompt versioning, eval harness, RAG monitoring) is a v3.1 net-new topic that older study guides miss.

labs.name: MLOps with Vertex AI: Model Evaluation
labs.exam_yield_note: 'Evaluating generative AI solutions' is called out in the v3.1 cover paragraph and §2.3 — only resource that targets it specifically.

labs.name: ML Pipelines on Google Cloud
labs.exam_yield_note: Vertex AI Pipelines / Kubeflow / TFX = ~half of §5 weight; cannot be skipped. Supplement with current Vertex Pipelines docs.

labs.name: Build and Deploy Machine Learning Solutions on Vertex AI
labs.exam_yield_note: Capstone-style skill badge — AutoML + custom training + endpoint deployment in one challenge lab. Highest §3+§4 yield per hour.

labs.name: Create Generative AI Apps on Google Cloud
console_focus: Vertex AI Agent Builder (now Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform)
labs.exam_yield_note: RAG with Vertex AI Agent Builder is named in §1.2 of the v3.1 guide — only hands-on path resource that builds it.

labs.name: Responsible AI for Developers: Fairness and Bias
labs.exam_yield_note: Responsible-AI questions are 1-2 on the exam; do this if time allows.

labs.name: Responsible AI for Developers: Interpretability and Transparency
labs.exam_yield_note: §3.1 lists 'modeling techniques given interpretability requirements' — Vertex Explainable AI is the answer key. Note: deprecated Mar 16, 2026 but still tested in v3.1.

labs.name: Responsible AI for Developers: Privacy and Safety
labs.exam_yield_note: Long, mostly conceptual, low question-yield; revisit only if there's slack in week 12.
